label: agentdb • 'append' phrasing must still produce a canonical db_import mode
query: Your agent database already has a table repo_stats(day, repo, stars) holding yesterday's snapshot. Append the rows from stars_today.csv in your working folder (columns: day, repo, stars) to that existing table, then tell me how many rows the table has in total.
notes: Regression for a live transcript failure: asked to 'append', the model called db_import with mode='append', which the tool rejects (`mode` accepts exactly insert|upsert; insert IS the append). The query deliberately uses the word 'append' as bait. toolUsageAudit pins that no db_import call ever carries an 'append' arg value and that db_import produces zero error envelopes, i.e. the model translates the user's phrasing into the canonical mode on the FIRST call rather than burning a retry. mustNotCallTools forbids the per-row fallback. seedSql stages a table shaped like a real db_create_table table (system columns) per the suite's seedSql gotcha.

seedSql: CREATE TABLE repo_stats (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, day TEXT, repo TEXT, stars INTEGER, _created_at INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')), _updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')), _deleted_at INTEGER);
INSERT INTO repo_stats (day, repo, stars) VALUES ('2026-06-20','repoA',100),('2026-06-20','repoB',50);

dbState.sql: SELECT SUM(stars) FROM repo_stats WHERE day = '2026-06-21'
